Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

List of K-pop songs on the Billboard charts is a compilation of weekly chart information for K-pop music published by the Billboard charts, and reported on by Billboard K-Town, an online Billboard column. This is a list of K-pop songs and singles, and songs performed by K-pop artists, on the Billboard charts.[1][2]

2001 – present

[edit]
  • This list depends on continual updates taken from *Billboard artist/title/chart search[3] and *Billboard charts.[4]
  • Charts with all updates 2009–present are marked (Complete).
  • Billboard artists comprehensive update incomplete.
  • Billboard charts comprehensive update incomplete.
  • The list is currently minus portions of Psy's performance on 58 charts.
  • The list is exclusive of Korea K-Pop Hot 100 data.
  • Figures in red highlight indicate the highest ranking achieved by a K-pop artist on that chart.
  •   – Current week's charting

Notes

[edit]
  1. ^ Peaked at No. 1 on 2026-01-31 (1 week)
  2. ^ 2020-10-03 (1 week), 2020-10-24 (2 weeks), 2021-01-16 (1 week)
  3. ^ 2020-10-17 (1 week)
  4. ^ a b c d e f g h i j k l m n o p q r s t u v w x y z aa ab ac ad ae af ag ah ai aj ak al am an ao ap aq ar as at au av aw ax ay az ba bb bc bd be bf bg bh bi bj bk bl bm bn bo bp bq br bs bt bu bv bw bx by bz ca cb cc cd ce cf cg ch ci cj ck cl cm cn co cp cq cr cs ct cu cv cw cx cy cz da db Entered at No. 1 for 1 week
  5. ^ a b c d e f g h i j k l Entered at No. 1 for 2 weeks
  6. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 7 weeks
  7. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 9 weeks, returned to No. 1 on 2025-01-11 (2 weeks), 2025-02-08 (1 week)
  8. ^ Peaked at No. 1 on 2025-07-19 (1 week) and on 2025-08-02 (11 weeks), 2025-11-01 (2 weeks), 2025-11-15 (3 weeks), 2025-12-13 (1 week)
  9. ^ a b c Entered at No. 1 for four weeks.
  10. ^ 2020-09-26 (3 weeks), 2020-10-24 (2 weeks), 2020-12-26 (1 week), 2021-01-09 (2 weeks)
  11. ^ Peaked at No. 3 on 2020-10-17 (2 weeks)
  12. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 5 weeks
  13. ^ a b c Entered at No. 1 for 3 weeks
  14. ^ Peaked at No. 1 on 2023-05-27 (1 week), 2023-06-03 (1 week)
  15. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 9 weeks
  16. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 9 weeks, returned to No. 1 on 2025-01-11 (8 weeks) and on 2025-03-29 (2 weeks); tied with Mariah Carey's "All I Want for Christmas Is You" as the longest-running #1 song for 14 weeks.
  17. ^ Peaked at No. 1 on 2025-07-19 (1 week) and on 2025-08-02 (11 weeks), 2025-11-01 (2 weeks), 2025-11-15 (3 weeks), 2025-12-13 (3 weeks)
  18. ^ 2020-10-17 (1 week)
  19. ^ Debuted at No. 1 (2 weeks), returned to No. 1 on 2020-10-03 (1 week)
  20. ^ a b c Debuted at No. 1 (1 week)
  21. ^ Debuted at No. 1 (7 weeks), returned to No. 1 on 2021-07-31 (2 weeks), 2021-09-11 (1 week, remix with Megan Thee Stallion not credited for rebound)
  22. ^ Debuted at No. 1 (1 week)
  23. ^ a b c d e Re-entered on its second week

  47. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 7 weeks, returned to No. 1 on 2021-07-31 (10 weeks), 2021-12-18 (1 week); remix with Megan Thee Stallion was not credited for status)

  49. ^ Entered at No. 1 (3 weeks), returned to No. 1 on 2023-12-16 (1 week), 2023-12-30 (1 week)

  92. ^ Entered at No. 1 for 12 weeks, the entire summer season
  93. ^ Streaming Songs chart started on Jan 19, 2013. And Psy's Gangnam Style stayed on the chart for 49 straight weeks from Jan 26 to Dec 28, 2013. Those 122 weeks shown in Psy's chart-history seem to include re-entries
  94. ^ On the 2021-09-04 chart, the Megan Thee Stallion remix was credited with a rebound to No. 35
  95. ^ Peaked at No. 1 on 2025-08-02 (13 weeks)
